steps.title: Identify the key temporal clue
steps.content:
The student’s claim stresses that modern irrigation methods have a lineage that long predates the 1890 completion of Searsville Lake.
Thus we need a quotation showing irrigation techniques existed well before that date and are still used today.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer describes the Hohokam irrigation system from the 7th century CE whose hydraulic engineering design principles are still utilized today, directly showing a pre‑1890 lineage.
The other three choices either discuss present‑day importance, post‑World War II growth, or late‑20th‑century sprinkler systems, none of which demonstrate an ancient lineage predating Searsville Lake.
That is the correct answer.
